widgetMetadata.instructions: Each entry under `widgets` describes one chart/widget rendered on this page of the Israeli Ecosystem dashboard. Descriptions are result-agnostic: they explain what the visualization shows and how to interpret it, not the specific numbers. The `dataPaths` array on each entry lists dot-notation paths inside this same JSON file where the underlying data for that widget lives (e.g. "kpis.patentingStartups" or "ipIntensity.items") When summarizing or answering questions about a number from this snapshot, locate the relevant widget(s) by `dataPaths` and quote the corresponding `description` to explain the metric. Widgets that have a 10 year trend usually show the total overall (across all years) and you get the exact data for each year in the past 10 years. The 10 year data do not add up to the total of course

business-models:kpi-identified-products.title: Identified Products
business-models:kpi-identified-products.description: Total number of product records associated with Israeli startups. The bar chart shows identified products by the founding year of the company that owns the product over the past 10 years.

business-models:kpi-saas-products.title: SaaS Products
business-models:kpi-saas-products.description: Total number of Israeli startup product records marked as SaaS. The bar chart shows SaaS products by the founding year of the company that owns the product over the past 10 years.

business-models:saas-mix-over-time.title: SaaS Mix Over Time
business-models:saas-mix-over-time.description: Share of Israeli startup product records marked as SaaS versus non-SaaS by the founding year of the company that owns the product. The white boundary line shows the SaaS share over the past 10 years, with SaaS below the line and non-SaaS above it.

business-models:top-product-groups.title: Top Product Groups
business-models:top-product-groups.description: Top 20 product groups among Israeli startup product records, ranked by product count. Bars are product-weighted, so companies with multiple products can contribute multiple rows.

business-models:market-motion-sankey.title: Market Motion Flow
business-models:market-motion-sankey.description: Company-weighted Sankey flow showing how Israeli startups move from industry to customer segment, operating model, scalability, and revenue model. Each company contributes once using its highest-priority product for product-level revenue fields; companies missing any displayed dimension are excluded from the flow rather than shown as analytical categories.

business-models:customer-type-profiles.title: Customer Type Profiles
business-models:customer-type-profiles.description: Five heatmap cards, one for each Sankey column. Rows are subgroups in that column and columns are the most common customer-type keywords for that dimension. Cell intensity represents the share of companies in the subgroup tagged with that customer type. Customer types are multi-label, so row shares can overlap and do not sum to 100%.

columns.label: Industry
columns.description: Broad market sector assigned to the startup.

columns.label: Customer segment
columns.description: Who primarily buys or uses the company offering: business, consumer, government, or mixed.

columns.label: Operating model
columns.description: The commercial and delivery physics behind the business, such as SaaS, hardware, marketplace, or milestone-driven deep tech.

columns.label: Scalability
columns.description: How the company scales customer acquisition and delivery, from self-serve to high-touch and services-led motions.

columns.label: Revenue model
columns.description: How the primary product appears to monetize: recurring, one-time, or usage-based.

nodes.name: IT and Telecom
nodes.description: Information technology, software, communications, and telecom infrastructure.

nodes.name: Healthcare and Food
nodes.description: Healthcare, life sciences, medical, food, and agriculture-related companies.

nodes.name: Media and Entertainment
nodes.description: Media, content, gaming, entertainment, and audience businesses.

nodes.name: Consumer Goods and Services
nodes.description: Consumer-facing products and everyday services.

nodes.name: Financial Services
nodes.description: Finance, insurance, payments, lending, trading, and related services.

nodes.name: Energy and Utilities
nodes.description: Energy, climate, infrastructure, utility, and resource systems.

nodes.name: B2B
nodes.description: Sells primarily to business customers.

nodes.name: B2C
nodes.description: Serves individual consumers directly.

nodes.name: B2B2C
nodes.description: Reaches consumers through business partners or intermediaries.

nodes.name: B2G
nodes.description: Sells primarily to government or public-sector buyers.

nodes.name: B2B Enterprise SaaS
nodes.description: Software sold to organizations, usually recurring and sales-led.

nodes.name: B2B Industrial Hardware
nodes.description: Physical products or systems sold into industrial or operational environments.

nodes.name: Hybrid Hardware SaaS
nodes.description: Physical product with a recurring software, data, or service layer.

nodes.name: Marketplace Transactional
nodes.description: Platform connecting sides of a market and monetizing transactions or activity.

nodes.name: Usage Based API
nodes.description: Developer or API-led product where revenue scales with usage.

nodes.name: Deep Tech Milestone
nodes.description: Deep-tech business where progress is often milestone, R&D, regulatory, or partnership driven.

nodes.name: Sales Led
nodes.description: Growth depends mainly on a direct sales motion.

nodes.name: Self Serve
nodes.description: Customers can adopt with limited human assistance.

nodes.name: High Touch
nodes.description: Requires consultative selling, onboarding, or delivery support.

nodes.name: Low Touch
nodes.description: Uses lightweight assisted sales or support after initial demand.

nodes.name: Services Led
nodes.description: Growth depends substantially on service delivery or implementation work.

nodes.name: Recurring
nodes.description: Revenue is primarily subscription or repeat-contract based.

nodes.name: One-time
nodes.description: Revenue is primarily one-time product or project purchase.

nodes.name: Usage-based
nodes.description: Revenue scales with consumption, transaction volume, or measured usage.

nodes.name: poa
nodes.description: Classified as poa within Revenue model.

nodes.name: free
nodes.description: Classified as free within Revenue model.

nodes.name: freemium
nodes.description: Classified as freemium within Revenue model.
